title: The Brain, Body, and Behaviour Dataset (1.0.0) - Experiment 4
description: The Brain, Body, and Behaviour Dataset (Experiment 4) is a multimodal neurophysiological dataset comprising simultaneous recordings of EEG, EOG, ECG, respiration, eye-tracking, and head position from 43 subjects across two sessions while viewing educational videos. Session 1 (attentive): subjects watched three instructional videos with focused attention and completed memory testing via multiple-choice questions after each video. Session 2 (distracted): subjects watched three different videos while performing a backward counting task (silent counting from a random prime number in steps of 7), with no memory testing. This dataset enables investigation of neural, physiological, and behavioral correlates of attention, learning, and cognitive engagement during multimedia instruction.
methods_description: Multimodal recordings were collected during two sessions: (1) attentive viewing of three educational videos (Stim 1, 2, 3) with memory testing via multiple-choice questions administered after each video, and (2) distracted viewing of three different videos (Stim 4, 5, 6) while performing a backward counting task. Recorded modalities included EEG, EOG, ECG, respiration, pupil size, gaze coordinates (X, Y), blinks, saccades, and head position (X, Y, Z). Raw data were converted from MATLAB .mat files to BDF format for EEG and TSV.GZ format for physiological and eye-tracking recordings.
